content.full_text:
May 7, 1975

75-05-133


Mr. John K. Facey
1002 Cypress Loop
Fortuna, CA 95540

Dear Mr. Facey:

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 75-107

Thank you for your letter of April 23, 1975
requesting an opinion with respect to your duties
under the Political Reform Act.

Since your request raises no substantial question
involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act,
your request for a formal opinion is denied. No ques-
tion arises under Government Code Section 87100 because
it does not seem likely that any action of either the
City of Fortuna Planning Commission or the Humboldt
County Department of Public Works would affect the
interests of the other. In any such case, however,
you would be required to disqualify yourself.

Although for the reason stated no formal opinion
will be issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently
helpful. If you have any further questions, I urge
you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,


Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

DHL:cjb


22

April 23, 1975

Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
926 "J" Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

I am employed in the Humboldt County Department of Public Works as Supervising Engineer, in charge of the Engineering Division. The Engineering Division consists of two Sections - Office Engineering and Field Engineering, and these sections perform the work generally related to these sections. In addition I am deputised to act as the Public Works Director in the absence of the Director and the Assistant Director. Attached is a copy of my job description.

Recently I have been asked by the City of Fortuna, the location of my residence, to serve as a member of the City Planning Commission.

Would you please issue an opinion as to whether or not I would be in violation of the Conflict of Interest provisions of Proposition No. 9 if I served on the City of Fortuna's Planning Commission.

Additional information will be furnished if needed.

Very truly yours,

John K. Facey
1002 Cypress Loop
Fortuna, California 95540

Attachment

County of Humboldt                                    (Nov.) December, 1971

                                                               Request No. ___________
                                                               Date Received _________
                                                               Response Due __________


                            SUPERVISING ENGINEER


Definition

     Under general direction to plan, supervise and coordinate the
activities of the engineering division of the Public Works Department;
and to do releated work as required.


Typical Tasks

     Supervises a professional and non-professional staff in prepara-
tion of preliminary and final engineering plans, specifications, cost
estimating, contract documents and other details for a wide variety of
public works projects; prepares divisional budget and assists supervisor
in departmental budget compilation; supervises engineering inspectors
involved in inspecting the construction of subdivisions, a wide variety
of public works projects under contract with F.A.S. projects; confers with
other divisions, departments, private surveyors and engineers and meets
with official governmental bodies in maintaining standards for subdivi-
sion construction; coordinates activities with other sections and depart-
ments, private engineers and surveyors, other agencies and the public;
solves difficult engineering problems; conducts comprehensive engineering
studies; prepares reports and makes recommendations.


Employment Standards

     Minimum Qualifications: Any combination of education and experience
equivalent to graduation from a college or university with a major in
Civil Engineering and five years of increasingly responsible varied Civil
Engineering experience of which the last two years shall be of professional Civil Engineering after receipt of a California registered Civil
Engineer certificate. Registration as a Professional Civil Engineer in
the State of California. Applicant must also possess a valid California
Class 3 Drivers License or its equivalent at time of application.

     General Qualifications: Knowledge of the principles of civil engineering;
knowledge of basic legal requirements governing public works engineering;
ability to supervise and coordinate the activities of an engineering divi-
sion; ability to use good judgment on difficult engineering problems;
ability to make comprehensive engineering studies, prepare reports and
make recommendations on major projects; ability to establish and maintain
effective relations with others.
